ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Danny Stokes for Rose Singleton. Joshua Sondheimer for Department of Health Care Services.

Mr. Stokes stated that he had been reimbursed for the amount of $823.30 and that there was an outstanding balance of $395.00 was the only amount still owed to him. Mr. Sondheimer stated that a detailed statement for the extraordinary compensation for the tax services was not provided and objected to the $150.00 request for services in connection with the sale of the property. Mr. Sondheimer stated that the bonding agency indicated that they were prepared to provide the funds to the Department of Health Services but that it has not happened yet. Mr. Stokes stated that he would not request a discharge of bond until payment has been received. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Continued To Aug-11-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joshua Sondheimer for Department of Health Care Services (Creditor). Danny Stokes for Rose Singleton (Administrator).

Mr. Stokes stated that Ms. Singelton needed to be removed, that there was no Successor Representative and that the Examiner suggested the State take on the roll of Successor Representative. In addition, Mr. Stokes stated that the were two accounts, one with a balance of $84,000.00 and the one in Atlanta with a balance of $10,000.00, that the income tax needed to be prepared and filed, that there was a bond issue. Mr. Sondheimer stated that the State would not object to Mr. Stokes being appointed as Successor Representative and that they would relieve Mr. Stokes from going after the bond company. Mr. Stokes stated that both accounts were frozen and that he filed a petition to be relieved as Counsel. The matter was continued to August 11, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the Final Account and the petition to close the estate. The Court stated that it could issue an order to the banks directing them to release the funds to Counsel's trust account and that Mr. Stokes could then close the estate. Mr. Stokes will fax information regarding the account numbers. The issue regarding recovery on the bond is in Mr. Sondheimer's court. Mr. Sondheimer stated that there was an error in the surcharge amount and that the correct surcharge amount should be $421,181.82.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Continued To Jun-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of The Estate
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Continued To Jun-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Danny Stokes for Rose Singleton. Joshua Sondheimer from the Attorney General's Office for the Department of Health Services. Craig Jorgensen for Bond Services of California.

Ms. Singleton was not present. Counsel presented oral argument to support their position with regard to the amount of surcharges against Ms. Singleton, the basis for the calculations of the surcharge, the interest rate, bad faith on the part of Ms. Singleton, Ms. Singleton's knowledge/lack of knowledge of the lien from the Department of Health Services, the amount of funds left in the Estate if any and compensation being requested by Mr. Stokes. Mr. Jorgensen stated that bond premiums in the amount of $200.00 were past due. The issue regarding surcharge against Ms. Singleton and the request for compensation for Mr. Stokes were taken under submission. Ms. Singleton's powers as Administrator of the Estate of Lloyd Sparrow are suspended. Status of closing of the Estate was set for May 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Stokes was advised by the Court to contact Ms. Hilton (Court Examiner) by fax or e-mail providing copies/cc to Mr. Sondheimer and Mr. Jorgensen regarding what may be needed to close the estate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Verified Petition For Surcharge Of Rose Singleton, Personal Representative
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2010 Text Ruling

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-13-2010 Continued To Feb-26-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joshua Sondheimer for Department of Health Care Services. Danny Stokes for Rose Singleton.

The Court informed Mr. Stokes that the Proof of Service filed on January 5, 2010 was not dated and stated that the Bonding Company had filed a Request for Special Notice on December 9, 2009. Mr. Stokes stated that the bank statments and a declaration regarding the Proof of Service had been filed this morning, that the Bonding Company had been given notice and that he had no success in reaching his Client. In addition, he stated that he did not know if there was any cash left in the Estate and that the last amount known was about $90,000.00. Mr. Sondheimer stated that the civil complaint was pending, that there was a default prove up hearing scheduled for February 9, 2010 and that he intended to get a judgment filed and address the surcharge issue. In addition, he stated that it was appropriate to issue a citation to the Personal Representative to appear and that the request for surcharges was in the petition filed on August 24, 2009. The matter was set for an evidentiary hearing on February 26, 2010 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Rose Singleton (Personal Representative) to appear and show why her powers should not be suspended for failure to account. The return date is February 26, 2010 at 9:30 a.m, in Dept. 204 to be heard at the same time of the evidentiary hearing. The Court suggests Mr. Sondheimer review the petition to see if it should be amended. Mr. Stokes is to amend his petition for fees/compensation. Mr. Stokes provided a current address for Ms. Singleton.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-30-2009 Continued To Jan-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal And The First And Final Account By Rose Singleton
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Off Calendar

Verified Petition For Order Compelling Personal Representative To File Inventory And Appraisal, Account, And Status Report; And For Surcharge
Off Calendar Nov-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Danny Stokes for Rose Singleton (Administrator). Joshua N. Sondheimer for Department of Heatlh Services (Petitioner).

The Court stated that the First and Final Account would have to be completed using what Counsel has at this time. Mr. Stokes stated that a lawsuit has been filed, that he didn't want to "submit" his client until she is served in Georgia, that he can't reach her, that he has called her everyday since the last hearing, but there still has been no return call. Mr. Sondheimer requested that the Court order the Personal Representative to account. Mr. Stokes stated that there was no order as to his client. The Court stated that it would sign the order directing the Personal Representative to file an Inventory and Appraisal and to file the First and Final Account using the state required forms/format. The petition was granted (subject to review of the order). The Inventory and Appraisal and the First and Final Account are to be on file by December 9, 2009 and if it is on file then counsel won't have to appear on that date. Mr. Stokes stated that an Inventory and Appraisal has been filed; there were two partial Inventories filed. The matter was continued to December 14, 2009 for status on the filing of the Inventory and Appraisal and the First and Final Account, if they are not on file by that date then Counsel and the Personal Representative are ordered to appear. Mr. Stokes stated he would get it on file by December 9, 2009.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Verified Petition For Order Compelling Personal Representative To File Inventory And Appraisal, Account, And Status Report; And For Surcharge
Off Calendar Oct-27-2009 Continued To Nov-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Danny Stokes for Rose Singleton (Personal Representative). Joshua Sondheimer for Department of Health Services.

Mr. Stokes stated that the matter was in a position to do a final account, that he sent the documents to the Personal Representative in September and that she was not responding. In addition, he stated that he intended to file a petition to withdraw as Counsel for lack of cooperation from his Client. Mr. Sondheimer spoke of having Ms. Singleton removed as the Personal Represtative. Mr. Stokes stated that Ms. Singleton had all of the assets with a minimal bond and that the property sold for $550,000.00. The matter was continued to November 3, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
